name: bigstream_registration
description: Runs a registration pipeline using Bigstream on a Dask cluster

outputs.name: global
outputs.description:
Global alignment results from BIGSTREAM_GLOBALALIGN.
Structure: [ val(meta), path(fix_image), val(fix_image_subpath),
             path(mov_image), val(mov_image_subpath),
             path(transform_dir), val(transform_name),
             path(align_dir), val(align_name), val(align_subpath) ]

outputs.name: local
outputs.description:
Local alignment results from BIGSTREAM_LOCALALIGN.
Structure: [ val(meta), path(fix_image), val(fix_image_subpath),
             path(mov_image), val(mov_image_subpath),
             path(affine_transform),
             path(transform_dir),
             val(transform_name), val(transform_subpath),
             val(inv_transform_name), val(inv_transform_subpath),
             path(align_dir), val(align_name), val(align_subpath) ]

outputs.name: cluster
outputs.description:
Cluster termination signal.
Structure: [ val(meta), val(empty_map) ]

name: bioformats2raw
description: Convert any Bioformats-compatible image to OME-Zarr format

inputs.name: ch_meta
inputs.description:
Channel of tuples containing a meta map, the input image, an 
output path where the OME-Zarr should be created, and a memo directory
where temporary files will be saved.
Structure: [ val(meta), path(input_image), val(output_path), val(memo_directory) ]

outputs.name: params
outputs.description:
Same as the input tuple but replaces output paths with the OME-Zarr that 
was created.
Structure: [ val(meta), path(input_image), val(zarr_output_path) ]

outputs.name: versions
outputs.description: File containing software versions

name: cellpose
description: Performs a 3-D segmentation using cellpose distributed on a dask cluster

tools.name: cellpose
tools.description: A generalist algorithm for cell and nucleus segmentation.

inputs.name: meta
inputs.description: Groovy map containing: id, input_image_dataset, output_image_name

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: Path to the image

inputs.name: image_subpath
inputs.description: Relative path to the image dataset if needed (for N5 or zarr containers)

inputs.name: dask_config
inputs.description: Optional path to the dask config. If this is not specified pass in as empty list.

inputs.name: models_path
inputs.description: Optional path to the cellpose models cache directory. If this is not specified pass in as empty list.

inputs.name: outputdir
inputs.description: Directory location of the output image

inputs.name: output_name
inputs.description: Name the generated output file(s).

inputs.name: dask_scheduler
inputs.description: Dask scheduler IP

inputs.name: cellpose_cpus
inputs.description: Number of cpus needed to run the main cellpose program

inputs.name: cellpose_mem_in_gb
inputs.description: Memory needed for the main cellpose program

outputs.name: meta
outputs.description: Groovy map containing: id, input_image_dataset, output_image_name

outputs.name: results
outputs.description: Tuple containing meta, input image, output dir

outputs.name: result_names
outputs.description: Tuple containing result names and extensions

outputs.name: versions
outputs.description: File containing software versions

name: cellpose
description: cellpose segments cells in images using GPU-accelerated deep learning

tools.name: cellpose
tools.description: cellpose is an anatomical segmentation algorithm written in Python 3 by Carsen Stringer and Marius Pachitariu

inputs.name: model
inputs.description:
Optional custom cellpose model file. When provided, passed as
--pretrained_model to cellpose. Pass [] (empty list) to use the
default model (cpsam in cellpose 4).

name: dask_prepare
description: Prepare work directory for running a Dask cluster

tools.name: dask
tools.description: Dask is a flexible library for parallel computing in Python.

inputs.name: dask_work_dir
inputs.description:
Parent directory of the cluster work directory. 
This is passed only for mapping purposes and it must exist.

outputs.name: cluster_work_fullpath
outputs.description: Full path to the cluster work directory

name: dask_start
description: Creates a Dask cluster and waits for it to be ready

inputs.name: meta_and_files
inputs.description:
Channel containing metadata and files that need to be accessible by the cluster.
Structure: [ val(meta), path(files)... ]

inputs.name: distributed
inputs.description: If true, create a distributed Dask cluster; if false, return empty context

inputs.name: dask_config
inputs.description: Path to Dask configuration file (optional)

inputs.name: dask_work_path
inputs.description: Path to Dask work directory where cluster files will be stored (optional)

inputs.name: total_workers
inputs.description: Number of total workers to start in the cluster

inputs.name: required_workers
inputs.description: Minimum number of workers required before the cluster is considered ready

inputs.name: dask_worker_cpus
inputs.description: Number of CPU cores allocated per worker

inputs.name: dask_worker_mem_gb
inputs.description: Memory in GB allocated per worker

outputs.name: dask_context
outputs.description:
Dask cluster context information. If distributed=true, contains cluster details;
if distributed=false, contains empty map.
Structure: [ val(meta), val(dask_info) ]
Where dask_info is a map containing:
  - scheduler_address: Address of the Dask scheduler
  - cluster_work_dir: Path to the cluster work directory
  - available_workers: Number of workers that joined the cluster

inputs.name: image_subpath

inputs.description: Optional path to the cellpose models cache directory. If this is not specified pass in as empty list.

inputs.name: model_name
inputs.description: The name of the cellpose model.

inputs.description: Directory location of the output image container

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: Name of the labels container created at the location specified by output_dir

inputs.name: labels_subpath
inputs.description: Relative labels dataset subpath

inputs.name: working_dir
inputs.description:
Location of the temporary files created during the segmentation. This must have enough space to hold the temporary labels 
before they are reassigned during the merge process

inputs.name: dask_scheduler
inputs.description: Dask scheduler IP

inputs.name: dask_config
inputs.description: Optional path to the dask config. If this is not specified pass in as empty list.

inputs.name: preprocessing_config
inputs.description:
Optional YAML file holding the configuration parameters for various preprocessing operations.
So far the only preprocessing available is a gaussian filter but there may be more in the future

inputs.name: logging_config
inputs.description: Optional log config.

inputs.name: cellpose_cpus
inputs.description: Number of cpus needed to run the main cellpose program

inputs.name: cellpose_mem_in_gb
inputs.description: Memory needed for the main cellpose program

outputs.description: Subpath to the input image dataset

outputs.name: output_label_images
outputs.description:
One or more image names containing segmentation results. If the output is a zarr or N5 container
there's only one output_label_image, but if the output type is a tiff and the segmented image is large
there can be multiple tiff files

outputs.name: output_labels_subpath
outputs.description: this is the labels dataset if the output is a zarr or N5 container

outputs.name: versions
outputs.description: File containing software versions

name: spaceranger_count
description: Module to use the 10x Space Ranger pipeline to process 10x spatial transcriptomics data

tools.name: spaceranger
tools.description:
Visium Spatial Gene Expression is a next-generation molecular profiling solution for classifying tissue
based on total mRNA. Space Ranger is a set of analysis pipelines that process Visium Spatial Gene Expression
data with brightfield and fluorescence microscope images. Space Ranger allows users to map the whole
transcriptome in formalin fixed paraffin embedded (FFPE) and fresh frozen tissues to discover novel
insights into normal development, disease pathology, and clinical translational research. Space Ranger provides
pipelines for end to end analysis of Visium Spatial Gene Expression experiments.

inputs.name: reference
inputs.description: Folder containing all the reference indices needed by Space Ranger

inputs.name: probeset
inputs.description: OPTIONAL - Probe set specification.

inputs.description: Number of GB of memory per worker core

inputs.description: Number of cores for the Spark driver

inputs.description: Memory specification for the Spark driver

outputs.name: spark_context

The tuple from input ch_meta with the spark_context map appended.
Structure: [ val(meta), [ files ], val(spark_context) ]

outputs.name: done
outputs.description: The Spark work directory that was terminated.

cluster processes.

a spark context map.

as directory name only (not full path).

name: xeniumranger_import_segmentation
description:
The xeniumranger import-segmentation module runs `xeniumranger import-segmentation`
to recompute Xenium Onboard Analysis outputs using external segmentation results.
It supports two execution modes mirroring the Xenium Ranger CLI: an image-based
mode that accepts nuclei and/or cell masks (TIFF/NPY) or GeoJSON polygons together
with optional coordinate transforms and unit definitions, and a transcript-based
mode that ingests Baysor-style transcript assignment CSV files plus visualization
polygons. Use the image-based inputs when providing label masks or polygons, or
switch to the transcript-based inputs when supplying transcript-level assignments
so the appropriate command-line arguments are passed to Xenium Ranger.

tools.name: xeniumranger
tools.description: Xenium Ranger is a set of analysis pipelines that process Xenium In Situ Gene Expression data to relabel, resegment, or import new segmentation results from community-developed tools. Xenium Ranger provides flexible off-instrument reanalysis of Xenium In Situ data. Relabel transcripts, resegment cells with the latest 10x segmentation algorithms, or import your own segmentation data to assign transcripts to cells.
